title: ‘If he scores 34 goals, I’ll eat my hat’: Radio host’s boast in spotlight after Morgan Geekie’s milestone goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morgan Geekie scored his 34th goal of the season in the Bruins' 6-3 loss to the Predators.
Radio host Mike Felger had doubted Geekie's scoring potential, saying he'd eat his hat if Geekie reached 34 goals.
A milestone goal by Geekie prompted Sports Hub producer Kevin Maggiore to remind audiences of Felger's promise.

title: Boston police search for man accused of assaulting students
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police are searching for Seitu Morel, 32, accused of assaulting students near TD Garden this week.
Morel allegedly attempted to steal a bag on a charter bus after the student assaults, according to police.
Morel is also wanted for a February 25 assault and unarmed robbery in the Cleveland Circle area of Brighton.

title: Early bookings are strong for Cape Cod summer rentals
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Cape Cod summer rentals are in high demand, with early bookings nearly 20% higher compared to last year.
WeNeedAVacation.com and Pretty Picky Properties report seven out of 10 summer weeks are over 50% booked by January.
Rental rates on Cape Cod are rising 3-6%, with properties near the water seeing the highest price increases.

title: Ordering DoorDash in Boston? Here’s how much more you’re really paying.
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston diners pay up to 80% more for delivery orders as third-party apps take up to 30% per order.
Some Boston restaurants increase menu prices on delivery apps to offset fees, leading to higher costs for customers.
Restaurant owners suggest pick-up or dining in to avoid delivery fees and maintain meal quality.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com invites readers to test their knowledge with a Greater Boston news quiz.
The quiz, compiled by General Assignment Editor Peter Chianca, explores recent trending stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Needle pickup requests in Boston surged 71% from 2022 to 2025, but are 17% below 2020 levels.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% increase in needle pickup requests, while Back Bay recorded a 73% increase since 2020.
Boston's mobile sharps team operates daily to address 311 requests and conduct proactive sweeps for needle disposal.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon to support the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo's participation honors her mother, whose battle with Alzheimer's affected their family's cherished kitchen memories.
Items like a yellow plastic pitcher evoke strong memories of her mother's nurturing cooking traditions.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to the Nashville Predators, continuing their road struggles with an 11-14-4 record.
Bruins are second in the Eastern Conference Wild Card race, just one point ahead of Columbus before the trade deadline.
GM Don Sweeney is hesitant about trading key assets, but remains open to offers enhancing the team's depth and future.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: U.S. Court of Appeals granted a stay of removal for Seamus Culleton, delaying his deportation pending review.
Culleton, detained in Texas for six months, overstayed his visa in 2009 and has a pending green card application.
Culleton was arrested in Saugus in September; his wife, Tiffany Smith, calls his detention 'heartbreaking.'

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough, early Tuesday morning.
The fire broke out around 3 a.m., with flames venting through windows; firefighters arrived shortly after it started.
The cause of the fire is under investigation, and significant damage was sustained to the home.
